package service

import (
	"fmt"
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Positive scenario for service_method_returns_transport_writer_or_gin_h: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Services that return `gin.H`, `http.ResponseWriter`, or response-writing helpers.
type CaseServiceMethodReturnsTransportWriterOrGinHModel struct {
	ID       string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
	TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
	Status   string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
	Amount   int    `gorm:"column:amount" json:"amount"`
}

type CaseServiceMethodReturnsTransportWriterOrGinHRepository struct {
	db *gorm.DB
}

type CaseServiceMethodReturnsTransportWriterOrGinHService struct {
	repo *CaseServiceMethodReturnsTransportWriterOrGinHRepository
}

func (s *CaseServiceMethodReturnsTransportWriterOrGinHService) GetResponse(id string) (gin.H, error) {
	var model CaseServiceMethodReturnsTransportWriterOrGinHModel
	if err := s.repo.db.Where("id = ?", id).First(&model).Error; err != nil {
		return gin.H{"error": "not found", "status": 404}, fmt.Errorf("record not found: %w", err)
	}

	return gin.H{
		"id":       model.ID,
		"tenant":   model.TenantID,
		"status":   model.Status,
		"amount":   model.Amount,
		"message":  fmt.Sprintf("Item %s retrieved successfully", model.ID),
	}, nil
}
